questions.question: Magnesium is a cofactor for:

options.B: Many enzymes including ATPase, kinases, and stabilizes DNA/RNA structure

questions.question: Ferritin is a protein involved in:

options.B: Iron storage in liver and spleen

questions.question: Protein denaturation is irreversible when:

options.B: Extensive conditions (high heat, extreme pH) cause aggregation and cannot be reversed
options.D: Urea at low concentration causes it

questions.question: Cobalamin (Vitamin B12) requires intrinsic factor for absorption in:

options.B: Ileum (intrinsic factor produced by parietal cells of stomach)

questions.question: Kwashiorkor is caused by:

options.B: Protein deficiency with adequate calorie intake

questions.question: Selenium is an essential mineral that functions as:

options.B: Component of antioxidant enzyme glutathione peroxidase

questions.question: Alpha keratin (protein of hair and nails) has:

options.B: Predominantly alpha helix structure with many disulfide cross-links

questions.question: Fluoride in teeth prevents dental caries by:

options.B: Converting hydroxyapatite to fluorapatite (more acid-resistant enamel)

questions.question: The secondary structure of proteins was described by:

options.B: Linus Pauling and Robert Corey (alpha helix and beta sheet)

questions.question: Copper deficiency can cause:

options.B: Anaemia and neurological problems (copper needed for ceruloplasmin, cytochrome c oxidase)

questions.question: Collagen is a structural protein that has:

options.B: Triple helix structure (three polypeptides wound together), rich in glycine, proline, hydroxyproline

questions.question: Vitamin E (tocopherol) functions as:

options.B: Antioxidant protecting cell membranes from lipid peroxidation

questions.question: Potassium deficiency (hypokalaemia) causes:

options.B: Muscle weakness and cardiac arrhythmias

questions.question: Vitamin B6 (pyridoxine) is important for:

options.B: Amino acid metabolism (transamination and decarboxylation reactions)

questions.question: The Sanger method determined the primary structure of:

options.B: Insulin (first protein to have its complete amino acid sequence determined, 1955)

questions.question: Essential amino acids? no. Replaced: Amino acids which cannot be synthesized by body and must be supplied through diet.

options.B: False

questions.question: Non-essential amino acids? yes. body can synthesize them.

options.B: False

questions.question: Primary structure of protein refers to its ______.

options.B: Alpha helix

questions.question: Tertiary structure of protein is absolutely necessary for many biological activities of proteins.

options.B: False

questions.question: Quaternary structure is seen in proteins having more than one polypeptide chain. Example? yes. Haemoglobin.

options.B: False
